steps.description: Click the "Skip" link at the bottom left of the Visual Studio Code welcome screen to bypass the sign-in process.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" icon in the Visual Studio Code left sidebar to open its extension panel.

Use the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P in Visual Studio Code to open the Command Palette from the "Setup VS Code" walkthrough interface, with the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension and onboarding options visible in the sidebar.
Type 'notifications: show' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input box at the top center of the interface.
Press the Enter key to select the "Notifications: Show Notifications" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Click the "Accept" button in the notification prompting to help Red Hat improve its extensions, within the Notifications panel in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Changelog" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit pop-up notification at the bottom right of the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion CHANGELOG.md file is opened.
Right-click on the "Preview: CHANGELOG.md" tab in the Visual Studio Code interface to open the context menu for the file tab.
Click the "Close" option in the tab context menu within Visual Studio Code's interface to close all tabs except the currently selected one.
Click the "Create a New Agent/App" button in the left sidebar of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit panel within Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option within the "New Project" dialog in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code. This selects the template for creating agents, chat bots, or web apps in Microsoft 365, as highlighted by the red crosshair.
Click the "General Teams Agent" dropdown option in the "Teams Agent or App Using Microsoft Teams SDK" selection dialog of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Tab" option in the "Teams Capability" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel in Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Default folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" dropdown to select "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects" as the project root location in Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Type the text 'testing' into the "Input an application name" search bar at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Press the Enter key to confirm the input "testing" in the "Input an application name" text field at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Confirm" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit popup dialog at the bottom right of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Search Extensions in Marketplace" input box in the Extensions sidebar of Visual Studio Code.
Type text: 'microsoft 365 agent toolkit' into the "Search Extensions in Marketplace" input field in the Extensions sidebar of Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension entry in the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace sidebar, selecting the extension for additional details or installation options. The interaction targets the extension list item identified by its app icon, name, and publisher ("Microsoft"), highlighted with a red crosshair at position (173, 139) under the "EXTENSIONS: MARKETPLACE" panel.
Click the "Uninstall" button for the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension in the Visual Studio Code Extensions Marketplace panel.
Click the "Restart Extensions" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension details view within Visual Studio Code's Extensions Marketplace.
@assertion microsoft 365 agents toolkit hasn't installed.
Click the close (X) button at the top-right corner of the Visual Studio Code window to close the application.
Click the File button in the "Walkthrough: Setup VS Code" tab at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface to close the walkthrough pane.
Click the "New Window" option in the "File" menu of Visual Studio Code, highlighted in blue, to open a new editor window.
Click the "File" menu in the top-left corner of the Visual Studio Code application window to open the file options dropdown.
Click the "Open Folder..." menu item in the File dropdown of Visual Studio Code, indicated by the highlighted selection and shortcut Ctrl+K Ctrl+O.
Click the "AgentsToolkitProjects" folder name in the VS Code file selection dialog, within the "Home" directory, to select it for opening.
Click the "Open" button in the lower-right corner of the file selection dialog to confirm folder selection in the vscode application interface.
Right-click on the "testing" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code, as indicated by the red crosshair at (123, 102), to open the context menu for folder operations.
Click the "Open in Integrated Terminal" option in the context menu for the "testing" folder in the VS Code Explorer panel.
Type the text `code .` into the integrated terminal at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code window, within the `AgentsToolkitProjects/testing` directory.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal after typing "code ." to execute the command for opening the current folder.
Click the gear icon for language and region settings in the bottom-left corner of the Visual Studio Code welcome screen.
@assertion there's notification "Do you want to install the recommended 'Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit' extension from Microsoft for this repository?"
Click the "Install" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension prompt at the bottom-right of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension entry in the left sidebar of the Visual Studio Code Extensions panel, selecting the extension for more details.

Click the "Use weaker encryption" button in the VS Code notification dialog prompting to choose an encryption method for storing secure data, as part of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension setup.
@assertion ACCOUNTS, ENVIRONMENT, DEVELOPMENT, LIFECYCLE, UTILITY sections exist.
